ending: Sen reaches the coast alone and accepts that he can no longer return to an anonymous wandering life. Wearing Caihong's blue robes and openly using the name Lu Sen, he books passage with a captain who carried him before. Two admiring sect siblings approach him before departure. Instead of encouraging their fascination with Judgment's Gale, he corrects their exaggerated stories, warns them that his path has brought pain, and gives them healing elixirs to use for others. He then sails for the ancient divine turtle's cove. Falling Leaf remains voluntarily with the nascent-soul alchemist to train with weapons, and her bond with Sen is intact. The alchemist still holds the remainder of Sen's five-year obligation even though he now possesses the manual. The royal princess is alive in the former companion's care, the formation commission remains pending, and the Ghost Panther claims on Falling Leaf are unsettled. Three cultivators struck by Heaven's Shadow remain severely traumatized under a core-stage investigator's care, creating a possible conflict with their sect that Sen does not yet know about.
in_short: Sen begins the volume close to death because his incomplete body cultivation is destroying him. He and Falling Leaf find the nascent-soul alchemist who owns the Five-Fold Body Transformation Manual, but she demands five years of service or observation. After retrieving rare mushrooms, surviving attacks, and discovering his unusual pill-refining method, Sen makes the dangerously potent pill required for his transformation and survives. He receives the manual, gains another core layer, and leaves Falling Leaf training with the alchemist while he visits the capital and then heads for the ancient divine turtle. In the capital he defeats the royal princess without killing her and arranges for formation supplies. On the road, an unwanted messenger and a plague-stricken village force him to reconsider his reflexive hostility. His new Heaven's Shadow technique spares three attackers but leaves them catastrophically impaired. Sen accepts the public identity he had tried to evade and sails toward the turtle's cove, while his debt to the alchemist, the Ghost Panther dispute, and possible sect retaliation remain unresolved.

recaps.text: Previously: Sen became an independent cultivator known publicly as Judgment's Gale, combining body cultivation, unusual qi abilities, and exceptional alchemical talent. Feng, Kho, and Caihong became his teachers and senior protectors, repeatedly restraining the most dangerous extremes of his training. Falling Leaf, a Ghost Panther able to pass for human, chose Sen and those teachers as her family and travelled beside him as both companion and hunting partner. Their bond is chosen family rather than simple convenience. Sen also formed ties in the capital, including a former companion who can relay messages and a royal princess whose relationship with him became strained. His earlier travels created unfinished business with an ancient divine turtle.

Sen and Falling Leaf search the wilderness for the Five-Fold Body Transformation Manual while his condition steadily deteriorates. Rest, liquid qi, and healing elixirs offer less relief as the tissues of his body begin to fail. Falling Leaf refuses to abandon him, and Sen refuses to divide the search because he will not expose her to danger alone. After months without success, a tide of spirit beasts attacks their fortified shelter. Sen, barely able to stand, unleashes a devastating qi technique to protect Falling Leaf and collapses. He awakens healed and separated from her in the home of the cultivator they sought. The supposedly mad nascent-soul cultivator reveals that her behavior was deliberate and confirms that she owns the manual. She saved Sen partly because she fears Feng's response to his death. Sen accepts her price: five years of service or observation in exchange for access to the manual. He refuses to include Falling Leaf in the obligation, though she may stay or leave and must protect the cultivator's secrecy.

The nascent-soul cultivator delays giving Sen the manual until he has recovered. Over several months he regains much of his strength, though he remains below his former physical condition. She tests his ability to manage several kinds of qi and then sends him to retrieve three superior air-attributed dusk mushrooms from a mountain, allowing only three months. Falling Leaf accompanies him. On the road, Sen hears distorted stories about Judgment's Gale and later confirms that the feared name belongs to him after a conflict with Swift River cultivators. He and Falling Leaf initially spare and disarm four attackers. When their sect sends pursuit, Sen kills the leader but gives the survivors a face-saving account that may prevent escalation. Near the mountain, an elder Ghost Panther secretly observes the pair and then claims authority over Falling Leaf, demanding that she join a wider conflict among their kind. Falling Leaf rejects lineage as a basis for obedience. She names Sen, Feng Ming, Kho, and Caihong as her chosen pride. Sen prepares to resist, and the elder withdraws, leaving the larger Ghost Panther crisis unresolved.

Sen and Falling Leaf continue up the mountain under near-constant attack. An intrusive sect-affiliated cultivator follows them, claims superior cultivation and knowledge of Sen's past, but refuses to explain his purpose. Sen drives him away without killing him. The pair then finds the concealed hollow containing the dusk mushrooms. A giant spider poisons Falling Leaf, and Sen burns through the colony to carry her out and treat her with one of Caihong's healing pills. While Falling Leaf remains unconscious, a self-aware spider matriarch leads thousands against Sen's fortified camp to avenge her colony. Sen demonstrates overwhelming force but permits the survivors to retreat. Falling Leaf recovers fully, and the pair begins the return journey with the required mushrooms. They help a village by killing its bandit tormentors, and Falling Leaf agrees that they might someday live near ordinary people so she can learn their ways at her own pace. Before reaching safety, a coordinated force of spirit beasts attacks, including a young dragon. Sen kills the dragon and destroys the attackers, but is badly wounded and nearly emptied of qi. He and Falling Leaf recover in hiding, unable to identify who organized the assault.

Sen and Falling Leaf return after three months with the dusk mushrooms. The nascent-soul alchemist orders Sen to make a pill from them, only to discover that he has never learned conventional pill refining and has always made elixirs in an ordinary pot. His elixirs prove unusually good, but he repeatedly fails at even the simplest pill despite following the primer correctly. The alchemist suspends his deadline and joins the investigation. After hundreds of failed variations, Sen recognizes that his relief at leaving the workshop has hurt her. He finds her a rare ice-qi lotus, explains that he wanted distance from failure rather than from her, and repairs their working relationship. Falling Leaf also becomes more independent, visiting a town alone and matching Sen in a demanding spar. Sen finally abandons rigid recipe execution and applies the responsive intuition he uses for elixirs. This produces a successful healing pill. Repeated attempts confirm both the breakthrough and a new danger: his pills are consistently much stronger than intended. Because the Five-Fold Body Transformation requires Sen to make his own exacting pills, he must learn to reduce their potency before risking the process. The alchemist also agrees to find suitable training for Falling Leaf.

Sen cannot make his pills reliably weaker, and the stabilization holding his damaged body together begins to fail. With perhaps only weeks left, he stops experimenting and rushes through the remaining primer. He completes its final, exceptionally difficult pill and collapses. The nascent-soul alchemist then confirms that this dangerously potent result is the actual pill needed for the Five-Fold Body Transformation and gives him only even odds of survival. Falling Leaf's certainty that he will live gives Sen the resolve to proceed. Away from the shelter, the pill tears down and rebuilds his body. Sen remains conscious through sword practice and his trust in Falling Leaf, completes the transformation, and survives without a heavenly tribulation. His new strength and speed greatly exceed his old limits, though he needs time to control them. The alchemist finally gives him the manual, and he makes copies with the long-term hope of widening access. His five-year obligation to her remains in force. Sen decides to travel to the ancient divine turtle, stopping first in the capital. Falling Leaf chooses to remain with the alchemist for weapon training. Before leaving alone, Sen also forms another layer of his core without a tribulation.

Sen enters the capital quietly and visits his former companion after two years without word. She is angry at his silence but relieved that he survived, and tells him that both the royal princess and the earlier sect-affiliated visitor have been seeking information about him. Sen asks her to pass messages to Feng, Kho, and Caihong. The princess then arrives with six cultivators. Sen kills the most aggressive attacker, frightens the unwilling participants away, and refuses to kill them once they abandon the fight. The princess accuses him of murdering her father and attacks. Sen denies the act, avoids her strikes, and disarms her without injury when her rage threatens civilians. He leaves her alive in his former companion's care. Sen purchases temporary formation flags from an independent artisan and commissions specialized replacements, offering future alchemical help and advancement aids in exchange for supplies and possible instruction. Feng's watcher confronts the princess and warns her against another attack. She later drives away an unwanted visitor, offers to accompany Sen, and accepts his message for Feng. Sen strengthens the formations protecting her home, declines her offer, and departs south alone before the capital's powerful factions can impose new obligations.

On the road south, a messenger bypasses Sen’s formations and presses an unnamed master’s proposal. Sen refuses to hear it, and the encounter becomes a duel. The messenger is a skilled body cultivator and swordsman, but Sen defeats and spares him. The man follows for two days demanding an honorable death until Sen rejects his idea of honor as an excuse for further violence. Their dispute pauses when Sen finds a village devastated by disease. Most residents are already beyond recovery, so Sen tends the dying, burns bodies, and offers relief while saving only three young villagers. The messenger helps under his direction and admits that his master’s need cannot compare with this suffering. After a week, Sen leaves the six survivors and resumes his journey alone. Grief changes his outlook: he rejects both reflexive hostility and unguarded trust. When three cultivators threaten him, he creates Heaven’s Shadow, a nonlethal sword technique that forces them to experience emotions they have suppressed. Later, a core-stage investigator finds all three alive but catastrophically traumatized beneath the snow. She moves them to safety and may have to report a technique whose source witnesses regard as inhuman. Sen remains unaware of the possible conflict with her sect.

As Sen nears the coast, ordinary towns help him regain emotional balance after the plague village. He accepts that his sorrow will remain and decides to approach future strangers with wary judgment instead of automatic hostility. He also abandons the hope of returning to an uncomplicated, anonymous life. Wearing the blue robes Caihong gave him, he openly accepts the identity and reputation he once tried to avoid. At the harbor, Sen finds a captain and crew who carried him before and arranges passage to the ancient divine turtle's cove. Before departure, two young sect siblings follow him because they admire the stories surrounding Judgment's Gale. Sen corrects the exaggerated tales and explains that his achievements have also brought pain. He gives each sibling a high-quality healing elixir, encouraging them to help others rather than imitate his violence. Sen then boards the ship and sails toward his appointment with the turtle. Falling Leaf remains by choice with the nascent-soul alchemist for weapon training. Sen still owes the alchemist the balance of their five-year bargain, and the Ghost Panther dispute remains open. He does not know that Heaven's Shadow has left three cultivators severely impaired or that their sect may respond.
